skill.name: teach
skill.description: Create a persistent teaching workspace with missions, lessons, learning records, references, and curated resources.
skill.summary: Turns a workspace into a guided, stateful learning environment.
skill.author: mattpocock

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged many Markdown backticks, placeholders, and example links as command execution, weak cryptography, or reconnaissance. Review found no executable code, no prompt injection attempt, and no evidence of malware; the remaining risks are expected workspace file writes and optional external resource discovery.

low_findings.title: Workspace Filesystem State Management
low_findings.description: TRUE_POSITIVE low risk: the skill instructs the agent to create and update local workspace files such as lessons, learning records, resources, mission notes, and user preferences. This is core functionality, but users should expect persistent local files to be read and written in the current workspace.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The referenced lines directly describe reading, creating, and updating files in the workspace. The behavior is intentional and local, with no evidence of destructive or hidden file access.

low_findings.title: External Resource And Community Discovery
low_findings.description: TRUE_POSITIVE low risk: the skill asks the agent to gather trusted resources, cite external links, and identify communities. This can involve network access or user navigation to third-party sites, but the reviewed files show no credential collection or automated exfiltration.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The text explicitly encourages external citations and community recommendations. The risk is limited because examples are ordinary resource links and there is no code that sends data outward.

low_findings.title: Static Command And Cryptography Alerts Are Markdown False Positives
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE: the static analyzer treated Markdown backticks, fenced examples, filenames ending in .md, and ordinary prose as Ruby shell execution, weak cryptography, or reconnaissance. No executable scripts, shell commands, cryptographic operations, or system probing instructions were found in these locations.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review confirmed the flagged content is Markdown formatting and instructional text. There is no interpreter context or command execution primitive in the reviewed files.

content.value_statement: Learning across sessions often loses context and direction. This skill records goals, lessons, resources, and learning evidence in one workspace.
actual_capabilities: Creates a mission document that anchors learning goals and constraints.
Generates self-contained HTML lessons in a lessons directory.
Records demonstrated learning in sequential learning-record files.
Maintains curated resources with notes about when to use each source.
Builds glossary and reference documents for repeated review.
Uses prior records to choose lessons within the learner's current level.
limitations: It relies on the agent and user to verify external resources.
It does not include its own grading engine or assessment database.
It is designed for one learning mission per workspace.
It may create several local files during normal use.

use_cases.title: Learn a technical skill over time
use_cases.description: Structure lessons, references, and learning records while studying a programming language or framework.

use_cases.title: Turn research into guided practice
use_cases.description: Convert trusted sources into focused lessons with exercises, citations, and review material.

use_cases.title: Maintain a coaching workspace
use_cases.description: Track a learner's goal, constraints, progress, terminology, and next lesson choices.

prompt_templates.title: Start a Learning Mission
prompt_templates.prompt: Teach me React state management. First, help me define my mission and create the learning workspace files.

prompt_templates.title: Create the First Lesson
prompt_templates.prompt: Use my mission and resources to create a short lesson that teaches one practical skill I can practice today.

prompt_templates.title: Record What I Learned
prompt_templates.prompt: I completed the lesson and can explain the main concept. Add a learning record and update any glossary terms I now understand.

prompt_templates.title: Plan the Next Teaching Step
prompt_templates.prompt: Review my mission, learning records, resources, and references. Choose the next lesson based on my current level and explain why.

output_examples.input: Teach me enough SQL to analyze product usage data.
output_examples.output: The skill first clarifies the learner's real goal, then creates a mission focused on product analytics. It proposes the first lesson on selecting, filtering, and grouping usage events.

output_examples.input: I already understand joins, but window functions confuse me.
output_examples.output: The skill records the prior knowledge, avoids reteaching joins, and designs a targeted lesson on one window function pattern with a short practice task.

output_examples.input: Find better resources for learning strength training programming.
output_examples.output: The skill updates the resources file with trusted books, articles, and communities. Each entry includes what it covers and when to use it.

best_practices: Start by defining a concrete mission before asking for lessons.
Keep each lesson focused on one skill with a clear practice step.
Record learning only after the learner demonstrates understanding.
anti_patterns: Do not use one workspace for unrelated learning goals.
Do not treat resource lists as trusted without checking source quality.
Do not create learning records for material that was only mentioned.

faq.question: Does this skill teach any topic?
faq.answer: Yes, but it works best when the topic has clear goals, trusted resources, and practice tasks.

faq.question: Does it use local files?
faq.answer: Yes. It stores missions, lessons, records, notes, resources, and references in the current workspace.

faq.question: Can it browse the web for resources?
faq.answer: It can guide resource discovery when the agent has network tools. Users should still verify source quality.

faq.question: Can I use it with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code?
faq.answer: Yes. The report marks it as compatible with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: Does it replace a human teacher?
faq.answer: No. It structures learning and practice, but human feedback may still be needed for complex skills.

faq.question: What makes it stateful?
faq.answer: It keeps mission, progress, terminology, and resources as files that future sessions can read.
